text.title: Six phases from idea to validated production
text.summary: Fixed phase order: research (parallel) â†’ brainstorm â†’ SDD plan â†’ implement â†’ deploy â†’ validate. A phase advances only when its outputs exist on disk and `state.md` reflects the transition. Each phase invokes its sibling /faion workflow by name.

section.title: Phase 1 â€” Research (parallel)
section.p: Trigger: bootstrap complete, no `research/*.md` files exist yet.

What it does: dispatches one subagent per research dimension (each task brief in `tasks/NN-<slug>.md`). Subagents run in background (`run_in_background: true`) and save outputs to `research/NN-<slug>.md` before returning summaries.
Output: every dimension covered by a research file on disk. The orchestrator advances when all expected files exist; missing files trigger re-dispatch in Phase 5 of the cron loop.

rule.statement: Phase 1 task briefs MUST list the `/faion` knowledge resources the subagent should consult (e.g. `software-architect`, `gtm-strategist`, `rag-engineer`) and the search tool chain (Bash gemini â†’ codex â†’ claude as fallback) inside the brief itself, not in the dispatch message.
rule.rationale: The brief is the contract. Putting tool chains and knowledge paths in the dispatch couples the orchestrator to per-task content; putting them in the brief lets future re-dispatches reproduce the same subagent without orchestrator memory.

section.title: Phase 2 â€” Brainstorm
section.p: Trigger: all `research/*.md` files exist; no `brainstorm.md` yet.

What it does: a single subagent invokes `../brainstorm/` (multi-agent diverge â†’ converge â†’ review). Inputs: every `research/*.md`. Output: `brainstorm.md` with locked architectural decisions (stack, runtime shape, knowledge schema, billing model â€” project-dependent). Each locked decision is appended to `decisions/decisions.md`.

section.title: Phase 3 â€” SDD Plan
section.p: Trigger: `brainstorm.md` exists and decisions are appended.

What it does: per feature identified in brainstorm, the orchestrator creates `features/NN-<slug>/` and dispatches one planning subagent. Each subagent reads `brainstorm.md` + `decisions/decisions.md` and writes the SDD quartet: `spec.md`, `design.md`, `test-plan.md`, `implementation-plan.md`.
Output: complete SDD set per feature, ready for wave execution.

section.title: Phase 4 â€” Implement
section.p: Trigger: SDD quartet exists for at least one feature.

What it does: hands off to `../sdd-batch-orchestrator/` for wave execution. Each task subagent reads its `TASK_*.md`, executes, runs the project's pre-commit hook, and produces a granular commit (one logical change per commit).

rule.statement: Pre-commit hook failures MUST be fixed at the root cause and a NEW commit created â€” never `--amend`, never `--no-verify`.
rule.rationale: The workspace standard is that hooks are correctness gates, not noise. `--amend` after a hook failure rewrites a commit that did not happen; `--no-verify` ships broken artifacts that a later session must debug.

section.title: Phase 5 â€” Deploy
section.p: Trigger: Phase 4 produced merged commits and the project's deploy script exists.

What it does: invokes the project's `deploy-gh.sh` (or equivalent) following the workspace pattern (push â†’ SSH â†’ pull â†’ build â†’ deploy). DNS via Cloudflare API when needed (creds in 1Password).

rule.statement: Phase 5 MUST request operator approval via the platform user-choice primitive before the deploy step runs. Deploy is irreversible / shared-state; the orchestrator is not authorized to act unilaterally.
rule.rationale: Deploy mutates production. The umbrella's autonomy ends at the irreversible-action gate; the operator is the security boundary for shared-state writes.

section.title: Phase 6 â€” Validate
section.p: What it does: end-to-end walk of the product surface (per `test-plan.md`); adversarial probes on isolation boundaries (read-only); fallback-chain test (e.g. force primary tool failure â†’ secondary â†’ tertiary); cost-per-action measurement against the margin model.
Output: validation report appended to `state.md`. On full pass, `state.md` reads `phase: shipped` and the cron loop stops driving.

rule.statement: A phase advances if and only if (a) every output file the phase declares exists on disk AND (b) `state.md` records the transition with the new phase id and timestamp. Both conditions are required; either alone is insufficient.
rule.rationale: Files-only advance allows a half-written brainstorm to count as Phase 2 done. State-only advance allows a tick to claim progress without artifact. Coupling the two makes the loop restartable from disk after compaction.
